3e26c7cbfdfc84e3bf64c3f19e059f51ec698c9c.lexicon: [Particle: Muon Neutrino], [The muon neutrino is a second-generation neutral lepton with mass <0.17 MeV. It is produced in muon decay and pion decay. Muon neutrinos interact only via the weak force. Symbol: nu_mu. They participate in neutrino oscillations with electron and tau neutrinos.]

296563d91bddd25b07b84cd00cd02681bb99e6e8.lexicon: [Particle: Tau Neutrino], [The tau neutrino is a third-generation neutral lepton with mass <18.2 MeV. It is produced in tau lepton decay. Tau neutrinos interact only via the weak force. Symbol: nu_tau. First directly detected in 2000 by the DONUT experiment at Fermilab.]

e2fbaaab39fd7ab3fd84e9a54dda64b24421d8b0.lexicon: [Particle: W+ Boson], [The W+ boson is a massive gauge boson that mediates the weak nuclear force. It has charge +1, mass 80379 MeV (80.379 GeV), and spin 1. The W boson is responsible for beta-plus decay and other weak interactions. It was discovered at CERN in 1983. The W+ and W- bosons are antiparticles of each other.]

975e7c0bfd4cf6b0289258d202c1d675c5ac3d2d.lexicon: [Particle: W- Boson], [The W- boson is a massive gauge boson that mediates the weak nuclear force. It has charge -1, mass 80379 MeV (80.379 GeV), and spin 1. The W- boson is responsible for beta-minus decay. It is the antiparticle of the W+ boson. Together the W+, W-, and Z bosons are the carriers of the weak interaction.]

906e06cf24dd56bab73cf249773b53784d1e04bc.lexicon: [Particle: Z Boson], [The Z boson is a massive neutral gauge boson that mediates the weak nuclear force. It has zero charge, mass 91187 MeV (91.187 GeV), and spin 1. The Z boson mediates neutral current weak interactions and was discovered at CERN in 1983 alongside the W bosons. Symbol: Z0.]

ae4b3398ec1f3087758a7991f7add7b854850819.lexicon: [Particle: Graviton], [The graviton is the hypothetical massless gauge boson that would mediate the gravitational force in a quantum theory of gravity. It has zero mass, zero charge, and spin 2. The graviton has not been directly detected but is predicted by quantum gravity theories. It would travel at the speed of light.]

f0d30b1e918c1255fd3ec8068f60bbd34fe71908.lexicon: [ Behold a Square Zoom Ratio ], [ The zoom ratio required to complete the 'Behold a Square' meme arc into a full circle. Represents a 1.56% (1/64) distortion from the UBP Harmonic. ]

4b1f3194a928c7598b547680f5eb57d54aaf7e15.lexicon: [ GEO_CON_001 ], [ A 5-node tetrahedral manifold representing the geometric integration of Spatial, Geometry, Concept, Belief, and Memory. Serves as a template for stable semantic-logical bridging. ]

7aeac3e9e716c0b607d1570ca469153090b7da32.lexicon: [ Aqueous Bond ], [ The 8-bit stable octad operator for Hydrogen Bonding. Resolves H2O and stabilizes NH3. ]

4c67707e6ff9be6c65d4d8f752d79638a3fc5d6c.lexicon: [ Law of Bond Taxonomy ], [ Classification of existence tiers: Octads (W=8) are anchors, Dodecas (W=12) are dynamic, Hexadecas (W=16) are complex. ]

b5790f98b948b87ddc98dce4a61e207ea0bd5c38.lexicon: [Particle: Positron], [The positron is the antiparticle of the electron. It has charge +1, mass 0.511 MeV, and spin 1/2. When a positron meets an electron they annihilate, producing two gamma-ray photons each of 0.511 MeV. Positrons are produced in beta-plus decay and pair production. Symbol: e+.]

cd371cabc1d1ed056208d8010680deaecbbfc014.lexicon: [Particle: Antiproton], [The antiproton is the antiparticle of the proton. It has charge -1, mass 938.272 MeV, and spin 1/2. Antiprotons are produced in high-energy particle collisions and are used in antihydrogen synthesis. When an antiproton meets a proton they annihilate. Symbol: p-.]

8878d6f34b9891ed30a5e4f19f79c7d5beb717b1.lexicon: [Particle: Pion Plus], [The pi-plus meson (pion) is the lightest meson, composed of an up quark and an anti-down quark. It has charge +1, mass 139.57 MeV, and spin 0. Pions are the primary mediators of the residual strong force between nucleons. The pi-plus decays to a muon and muon neutrino with a lifetime of 2.6e-8 seconds.]

61f89bea68b4b8d83a9532d8d11392f8aa40f6f6.lexicon: [Particle: Pion Zero], [The neutral pion is a meson composed of a superposition of up-antiup and down-antidown quark pairs. It has zero charge, mass 134.98 MeV, and spin 0. The neutral pion decays to two photons with a very short lifetime of 8.5e-17 seconds, making it the shortest-lived hadron.]

7389e8e38d2da9e1ca0da26a4a3eb6c3b85ca0ba.lexicon: [Particle: Kaon Plus], [The K-plus meson (kaon) is a strange meson composed of an up quark and an anti-strange quark. It has charge +1, mass 493.68 MeV, and spin 0. Kaons carry strangeness and decay via the weak force with a lifetime of 1.24e-8 seconds. Kaons were the first strange particles discovered.]

50a6c6b0adedc1996661f100fa351fb3934f01d3.lexicon: [Particle: Lambda Baryon], [The lambda baryon is the lightest strange baryon, composed of up, down, and strange quarks (uds). It has zero charge, mass 1115.68 MeV, and spin 1/2. The lambda decays via the weak force with a lifetime of 2.63e-10 seconds, producing a proton and pion. It carries strangeness -1.]

da3955cebf43b7969d887defd8293e9efb7ef9fe.lexicon: [Particle: Alpha Particle], [The alpha particle is a helium-4 nucleus consisting of two protons and two neutrons. It has charge +2, mass 3727.38 MeV, and spin 0. Alpha particles are emitted in alpha decay of heavy nuclei. They are highly ionising but have low penetrating power. Symbol: alpha or He-4 nucleus.]

275104595cd20495469220e2e5bc348ce3c00020.lexicon: [Particle: Deuteron], [The deuteron is the nucleus of deuterium (heavy hydrogen), consisting of one proton and one neutron. It has charge +1, mass 1875.61 MeV, and spin 1. The deuteron is the simplest nuclear bound state and is stable. It is used in nuclear fusion reactions and as a neutron source.]

f66bb88c4503c7e551c622368066c0844bf5da41.lexicon: [Molecule: ATP], [Adenosine triphosphate (ATP) is the primary energy currency of the cell. It consists of an adenine base, a ribose sugar, and three phosphate groups. ATP releases energy when hydrolysed to ADP and inorganic phosphate. It is produced by ATP synthase in mitochondria via oxidative phosphorylation. Molecular formula: C10H16N5O13P3.]

88fd9602774666894f6f7e026c1169b1db70c000.lexicon: [Molecule: Adenine], [Adenine is a purine nucleobase with molecular formula C5H5N5. It is one of the four nucleobases in DNA (pairs with thymine) and RNA (pairs with uracil). Adenine is also a component of ATP and NAD+. It has a bicyclic structure consisting of a pyrimidine ring fused to an imidazole ring.]

c866bab3ae9fdeba7aa5b1074322b04661e38f26.lexicon: [Molecule: Glucose], [Glucose (dextrose) is the primary monosaccharide fuel for cellular respiration. Molecular formula C6H12O6, molar mass 180.16 g/mol. It is a hexose sugar that is phosphorylated in glycolysis to produce ATP. Blood glucose is tightly regulated at ~5 mM. Glucose is produced by photosynthesis and stored as glycogen or starch.]

a17f0401de0c2197b338815f74f28397cde8eff1.lexicon: [Molecule: Sucrose], [Sucrose (table sugar) is a disaccharide composed of glucose and fructose linked by a glycosidic bond. Molecular formula C12H22O11, molar mass 342.30 g/mol. It is the primary transport sugar in plants and the most widely used sweetener. Sucrose is hydrolysed to glucose and fructose by the enzyme sucrase.]

628d74b6af36e47a91994f0089a1775a80a6b643.lexicon: [Molecule: Cellulose], [Cellulose is a polysaccharide consisting of linear chains of beta-1,4-linked glucose units. It is the most abundant organic polymer on Earth, forming the structural component of plant cell walls. Molecular formula (C6H10O5)n. Cellulose is insoluble in water and resistant to digestion by most animals.]

b7f573eaad3fb0a55a7f15584502c86f2a8961a1.lexicon: [Molecule: Starch], [Starch is a polysaccharide consisting of alpha-1,4-linked glucose units (amylose) and alpha-1,6-branched chains (amylopectin). It is the primary energy storage carbohydrate in plants. Molecular formula (C6H10O5)n. Starch is digested by amylase enzymes to glucose. It forms a blue-black complex with iodine.]

82893d71dd8ff458244508e8e7a0e7845024e09e.lexicon: [Molecule: Ribose], [D-Ribose is a pentose monosaccharide with molecular formula C5H10O5. It is the sugar component of RNA, ATP, NAD+, and FAD. Ribose is produced in the pentose phosphate pathway. Unlike deoxyribose (in DNA), ribose has a hydroxyl group at the 2-prime carbon position.]

5a19f229940fc7fc19bdfabe70d21fd8295af008.lexicon: [Molecule: Deoxyribose], [2-Deoxy-D-ribose is a pentose sugar with molecular formula C5H10O4. It is the sugar component of DNA. Unlike ribose, deoxyribose lacks the 2-prime hydroxyl group, making DNA more stable than RNA. Deoxyribose is produced from ribose by ribonucleotide reductase.]

9b44acff531df90915ae120955e6868ed1d450fa.lexicon: [Molecule: Phosphoric Acid], [Phosphoric acid (H3PO4) is a triprotic inorganic acid with three ionisable protons. Molar mass 97.99 g/mol. It is a key component of DNA, RNA, ATP, and phospholipids. Phosphoric acid is produced industrially from phosphate rock and is used in fertilisers, food additives, and rust removal.]

ba73b23d071485376bcd81c7c681e3fc5abfcf16.lexicon: [Molecule: Nitrous Oxide], [Nitrous oxide (N2O) is a colourless gas with a slightly sweet odour. Molar mass 44.01 g/mol. It is used as an anaesthetic (laughing gas), a propellant in whipped cream dispensers, and a greenhouse gas. N2O is produced by soil bacteria during denitrification. It has 298 times the global warming potential of CO2 over 100 years.]

c050a650cea2ef42e929f33f67ce0cde5a3ca5f4.lexicon: [Molecule: Carbon Dioxide], [Carbon dioxide (CO2) is a linear triatomic molecule with molar mass 44.01 g/mol. It is produced by combustion, cellular respiration, and volcanic activity. CO2 is absorbed by plants in photosynthesis. It is the primary greenhouse gas responsible for climate change. At standard conditions it is a colourless gas. Solid CO2 is dry ice.]

4c644c67a424f873aa2a7485ff5787726ff174f7.lexicon: [Molecule: Carbon Monoxide], [Carbon monoxide (CO) is a colourless, odourless, toxic gas with molar mass 28.01 g/mol. It is produced by incomplete combustion of carbon-containing fuels. CO binds to haemoglobin with 200 times greater affinity than oxygen, causing carbon monoxide poisoning. It is also an important industrial reducing agent.]

8915715dfc571a4002b1097f75d71a060ed82ff6.lexicon: [Molecule: Hydrogen Sulfide], [Hydrogen sulfide (H2S) is a colourless, flammable gas with the characteristic smell of rotten eggs. Molar mass 34.08 g/mol. It is produced by the decay of organic matter and volcanic activity. H2S is toxic at low concentrations and is used in the synthesis of sulfuric acid. It is also a signalling molecule in biology.]

db8ed0110b448c600437ccfc4c139851c91e4f3b.lexicon: [Molecule: Methane], [Methane (CH4) is the simplest alkane and the primary component of natural gas. Molar mass 16.04 g/mol. It is produced by methanogenic archaea in anaerobic environments and by the decay of organic matter. Methane is a potent greenhouse gas with 84 times the warming potential of CO2 over 20 years. It is used as a fuel and chemical feedstock.]

0ce5508d5eef81c6f65a3f6f1390db370692a99a.lexicon: [Molecule: Ethanol], [Ethanol (ethyl alcohol, C2H5OH) is a volatile, flammable liquid with molar mass 46.07 g/mol. It is produced by fermentation of sugars by yeast. Ethanol is the active ingredient in alcoholic beverages, a fuel additive, and a solvent. It is metabolised in the liver by alcohol dehydrogenase. Boiling point 78.37 degrees C.]

d1c9538aa9c72b1f22108fa7cd7f321a0902870d.lexicon: [Molecule: Sodium Hydroxide], [Sodium hydroxide (NaOH, caustic soda, lye) is a strong base with molar mass 39.997 g/mol. It is a white solid that is highly soluble in water, producing an exothermic reaction. NaOH is used in soap making, paper production, drain cleaners, and chemical synthesis. It fully dissociates in water.]

ad878436459554b88b48753d510e6eb1cc323300.lexicon: [Molecule: Calcium Carbonate], [Calcium carbonate (CaCO3) is an ionic compound with molar mass 100.09 g/mol. It is the main component of limestone, marble, chalk, and shells. CaCO3 is used in cement, antacids, and calcium supplements. It decomposes on heating to calcium oxide and carbon dioxide. It is slightly soluble in water, more so in acidic conditions.]

9cd90e0a72515283b74c968526d605614448a953.lexicon: [Molecule: Iron(III) Oxide], [Iron(III) oxide (Fe2O3, haematite, rust) is the most common iron oxide with molar mass 159.69 g/mol. It is the main component of rust and the mineral haematite. Fe2O3 is used as a pigment (red ochre), in iron production, and as a magnetic material. It is a semiconductor with a band gap of 2.2 eV.]

48bde2807de3b5f49b37be9858b930200209a0d1.lexicon: [Molecule: Silicon Dioxide], [Silicon dioxide (SiO2, silica, quartz) is the most abundant mineral in the Earth's crust with molar mass 60.08 g/mol. It exists in many crystalline forms (quartz, cristobalite, tridymite) and as amorphous glass. SiO2 is used in glass, ceramics, semiconductors, and optical fibres. It is the main component of sand.]

a95a035e980c34265d2c900890db301dd88996eb.lexicon: [Molecule: Aluminium Oxide], [Aluminium oxide (Al2O3, alumina, corundum) is a white crystalline solid with molar mass 101.96 g/mol. It is the main component of bauxite ore and is used to produce aluminium metal. Corundum (Al2O3) is the second hardest natural mineral (Mohs 9). Ruby and sapphire are gem varieties of corundum coloured by trace impurities.]

50c72f89974e9d833d8524186a3d5fe4ed60d813.lexicon: [Molecule: Sodium Bicarbonate], [Sodium bicarbonate (NaHCO3, baking soda, bicarbonate of soda) is a white crystalline solid with molar mass 84.007 g/mol. It is a mild base used in baking (reacts with acids to produce CO2), as an antacid, fire extinguisher, and cleaning agent. It decomposes on heating to Na2CO3, CO2, and H2O.]

473f7289f60596743d71dfa0d3d27d85ac190af4.lexicon: [Molecule: Potassium Chloride], [Potassium chloride (KCl) is an ionic compound with molar mass 74.55 g/mol. It is a white crystalline solid highly soluble in water. KCl is used as a fertiliser, food salt substitute, and in medical saline solutions. It is essential for maintaining cellular membrane potential and is a key electrolyte in the body.]

865543cb8f87a8220d6d905cfe7170fd8d2cc88e.lexicon: [Molecule: Calcium Chloride], [Calcium chloride (CaCl2) is an ionic compound with molar mass 110.98 g/mol. It is highly hygroscopic and is used as a desiccant, de-icing agent, and food additive (E509). CaCl2 dissolves exothermically in water. It is used in medicine as a calcium supplement and in concrete acceleration.]

cacd677260bd45115ef3f8a55f46a766f53ff2ab.lexicon: [Molecule: Magnesium Oxide], [Magnesium oxide (MgO, magnesia) is a white crystalline solid with molar mass 40.30 g/mol. It has a very high melting point of 2852 degrees C and is used as a refractory material, antacid, and laxative. MgO has the rock salt crystal structure and is an ionic compound. It is produced by burning magnesium in air.]

4837801d23439dd50ea9d3c5ce6cb6e227cd61cd.lexicon: [Molecule: Nitrogen Dioxide], [Nitrogen dioxide (NO2) is a reddish-brown toxic gas with molar mass 46.01 g/mol. It is a major air pollutant produced by combustion engines and lightning. NO2 contributes to acid rain (forming nitric acid with water) and photochemical smog. It is used in the production of nitric acid via the Ostwald process.]

a2b8c62c5a21de34ef75c5eb2d3f54afe6f5efc2.lexicon: [Molecule: Sulfur Dioxide], [Sulfur dioxide (SO2) is a colourless, toxic gas with a pungent smell and molar mass 64.06 g/mol. It is produced by burning sulfur-containing fuels and volcanic eruptions. SO2 is a major cause of acid rain. It is used as a food preservative (E220), bleaching agent, and in the production of sulfuric acid.]

1f5e283bc46ebf58c01a3320364eaf3116481a49.lexicon: [Molecule: Chlorine Gas], [Chlorine (Cl2) is a yellow-green diatomic gas with molar mass 70.90 g/mol. It is a strong oxidising agent and highly toxic. Chlorine is used in water purification, bleach production, PVC manufacture, and as a chemical warfare agent in WWI. It reacts with water to form hydrochloric acid and hypochlorous acid.]

8503956a13f64964110d1745f4185e100e043908.lexicon: [Molecule: Fluorine Gas], [Fluorine (F2) is a pale yellow diatomic gas with molar mass 38.00 g/mol. It is the most electronegative and reactive element, capable of reacting with almost all substances including noble gases. Fluorine is used in uranium enrichment (UF6), Teflon production, and fluoride toothpaste. Extremely toxic and corrosive.]

4a4f309a10cbae77f6d2c0d9da202ce16dcda798.lexicon: [Molecule: Hydrogen Fluoride], [Hydrogen fluoride (HF) is a colourless gas or liquid with molar mass 20.01 g/mol. It is a weak acid in dilute solution but highly corrosive and toxic. HF is used in petroleum refining, glass etching, and semiconductor manufacturing. It penetrates skin and reacts with calcium in bones, causing severe chemical burns.]

4a312798feed0e99bf960e3a3dfe1da340eaedf4.lexicon: [Molecule: Hydrochloric Acid], [Hydrochloric acid (HCl in solution) is a strong mineral acid produced by dissolving hydrogen chloride gas in water. Molar mass of HCl: 36.46 g/mol. It is the acid in gastric juice (stomach acid) and is used in steel pickling, food processing, and chemical synthesis. Fully dissociates in water: HCl → H+ + Cl-.]

da4939c8f1bd15cc2778f5a5bd0827ba8df9df81.lexicon: [Molecule: Ammonia], [Ammonia (NH3) is a colourless gas with a pungent smell and molar mass 17.03 g/mol. It is produced by the Haber-Bosch process from nitrogen and hydrogen. Ammonia is the most widely produced industrial chemical, used primarily in fertiliser production. It is also a refrigerant, cleaning agent, and precursor to nitric acid. Biological nitrogen fixation also produces ammonia.]

b326312be5403cb1e82537c0862dce74ab27d4a1.lexicon: [Molecule: Hydrogen Gas], [Dihydrogen (H2) is the lightest and most abundant molecule in the universe. Molar mass 2.016 g/mol. It is a colourless, odourless, highly flammable gas. H2 is used in the Haber process for ammonia synthesis, petroleum refining, and as a clean fuel. It is produced by steam reforming of natural gas and electrolysis of water.]

4849d23489157b82a22ca6685590e2a586f6ca5b.lexicon: [Molecule: Oxygen Gas], [Dioxygen (O2) is a diatomic molecule with molar mass 32.00 g/mol. It is the second most abundant gas in Earth's atmosphere (21%). O2 is essential for aerobic respiration and combustion. It is produced by photosynthesis and used in steel production, medical oxygen therapy, and rocket propellants. It is paramagnetic due to two unpaired electrons.]

52ed01f76d869cf715f3868024fdbd83bbd60538.lexicon: [Molecule: Nitrogen Gas], [Dinitrogen (N2) is a diatomic molecule with molar mass 28.01 g/mol. It is the most abundant gas in Earth's atmosphere (78%). N2 has a very strong triple bond (bond energy 945 kJ/mol), making it chemically inert under normal conditions. It is used as an inert atmosphere in food packaging, electronics manufacturing, and cryogenics.]

789b3f60b0d5cadc55a0da945673b04a7cc53432.lexicon: [Molecule: Water], [Water (H2O) is the universal solvent and the most abundant liquid on Earth's surface. Molar mass 18.015 g/mol. It has an unusually high boiling point (100 degrees C) due to hydrogen bonding. Water is essential for all known forms of life, acting as a solvent, reactant, and temperature buffer. It has a bent molecular geometry with bond angle 104.45 degrees.]

5b0c3eacc0b7c499c08d20661783fd307fb89b6c.lexicon: [Molecule: Serine], [Serine (Ser, S) is a polar amino acid with molecular formula C3H7NO3 and molar mass 105.09 g/mol. It has a hydroxyl group on its side chain, making it a site for phosphorylation in cell signalling. Serine is involved in the biosynthesis of purines, pyrimidines, and cysteine. It is a non-essential amino acid.]

c3276212e1f7ef78eb920eca00ee43f5f48e88ab.lexicon: [Molecule: Threonine], [Threonine (Thr, T) is a polar essential amino acid with molecular formula C4H9NO3 and molar mass 119.12 g/mol. It has both hydroxyl and methyl groups on its side chain. Threonine is a site for O-linked glycosylation and phosphorylation. It is essential for protein synthesis and immune function.]

5888a4db57bc78050d5d28b23b3a19980b54ef6a.lexicon: [Molecule: Valine], [Valine (Val, V) is a nonpolar essential amino acid with molecular formula C5H11NO2 and molar mass 117.15 g/mol. It has a branched isopropyl side chain. Valine is one of the three branched-chain amino acids (BCAAs) along with leucine and isoleucine. It is important for muscle metabolism and tissue repair.]

08f5e257d452b7242573d121b15be7d7d1b84d63.lexicon: [Molecule: Leucine], [Leucine (Leu, L) is a nonpolar essential amino acid with molecular formula C6H13NO2 and molar mass 131.17 g/mol. It is the most abundant branched-chain amino acid (BCAA) and a key regulator of protein synthesis via the mTOR pathway. Leucine is essential for muscle protein synthesis and is found in high concentrations in meat, dairy, and legumes.]

b3d7593031273aeeb7feb984c99158c90c3921cc.lexicon: [Molecule: Isoleucine], [Isoleucine (Ile, I) is a nonpolar essential amino acid with molecular formula C6H13NO2 and molar mass 131.17 g/mol. It is a branched-chain amino acid (BCAA) and an isomer of leucine. Isoleucine is important for haemoglobin synthesis, energy regulation, and immune function. It has two chiral centres.]

69307b23d4b9b90301d02dcd2c6916876ba6cda7.lexicon: [Molecule: Proline], [Proline (Pro, P) is a cyclic amino acid with molecular formula C5H9NO2 and molar mass 115.13 g/mol. Its side chain is bonded back to the amino group, forming a pyrrolidine ring. Proline introduces rigidity into protein chains and is abundant in collagen. It is a non-essential amino acid synthesised from glutamate.]

12840173c1e3a971b8234f151b29d52d941b055e.lexicon: [Molecule: Methionine], [Methionine (Met, M) is a nonpolar essential amino acid with molecular formula C5H11NO2S and molar mass 149.21 g/mol. It contains a thioether group in its side chain. Methionine is the start codon amino acid (AUG) in protein synthesis. S-adenosylmethionine (SAM), derived from methionine, is the primary methyl group donor in biology.]

3917909259a2e59d865f60614bb48338ef85710a.lexicon: [Molecule: Lysine], [Lysine (Lys, K) is a positively charged essential amino acid with molecular formula C6H14N2O2 and molar mass 146.19 g/mol. Its long side chain ends in an amino group (pKa 10.53). Lysine is important for collagen cross-linking, calcium absorption, and carnitine synthesis. It is the limiting amino acid in cereal proteins.]

673a0afbd132152b922f3d814c3da8ed4ec63635.lexicon: [Molecule: Arginine], [Arginine (Arg, R) is a positively charged amino acid with molecular formula C6H14N4O2 and molar mass 174.20 g/mol. Its guanidinium side chain has pKa 12.48. Arginine is a precursor to nitric oxide (NO), a key signalling molecule. It is conditionally essential and is important for wound healing and immune function.]

55c720a1f696925518809df5d06336bb7ae37e5c.lexicon: [Molecule: Aspartate], [Aspartate (aspartic acid, Asp, D) is a negatively charged amino acid with molecular formula C4H7NO4 and molar mass 133.10 g/mol. Its side chain carboxyl group has pKa 3.65. Aspartate is a key intermediate in the urea cycle and purine synthesis. It is a neurotransmitter and the parent compound of asparagine.]

963bf2b166f82981c62049850eecb8dc33712db0.lexicon: [Molecule: Glutamate], [Glutamate (glutamic acid, Glu, E) is a negatively charged amino acid with molecular formula C5H9NO4 and molar mass 147.13 g/mol. It is the most abundant excitatory neurotransmitter in the brain. Glutamate is central to nitrogen metabolism and is the precursor of glutamine, proline, and arginine. It is non-essential.]

a8d33a51475f958f91d03fb9e55ecc45aa97526a.lexicon: [Molecule: Asparagine], [Asparagine (Asn, N) is a polar amino acid with molecular formula C4H8N2O3 and molar mass 132.12 g/mol. It was the first amino acid to be isolated (from asparagus in 1806). Asparagine is a site for N-linked glycosylation in proteins. It is non-essential and is synthesised from aspartate.]

9a350bec4b47e4e995ec928b21919833602eff95.lexicon: [Molecule: Glutamine], [Glutamine (Gln, Q) is a polar amino acid with molecular formula C5H10N2O3 and molar mass 146.15 g/mol. It is the most abundant free amino acid in the blood. Glutamine is the primary nitrogen carrier in the body and a fuel for rapidly dividing cells (intestinal cells, immune cells). It is conditionally essential.]

81ac73a88eb2e789aa8d7c738556c00dd80b1333.lexicon: [Crystal: Graphite], [Graphite is a crystalline allotrope of carbon with a layered hexagonal structure. Each carbon atom is sp2 hybridised and bonded to three others in flat sheets (graphene layers) held together by van der Waals forces. Graphite is soft (Mohs 1-2), electrically conductive, and used as a lubricant, electrode, and pencil lead. Density 2.09-2.23 g/cm3.]

0312212010e18f383eb4084774035957c1646403.lexicon: [Crystal: Quartz], [Quartz is a crystalline form of silicon dioxide (SiO2) with a trigonal crystal structure. It is the second most abundant mineral in Earth's continental crust. Quartz is piezoelectric and is used in oscillators, clocks, and electronic filters. It has a Mohs hardness of 7 and a refractive index of 1.544. Common varieties include amethyst, citrine, and rose quartz.]

86a5db5531516c46ae052abc02d9a8b205fcb88b.lexicon: [Crystal: Calcite], [Calcite is a crystalline form of calcium carbonate (CaCO3) with a trigonal crystal structure. It is the most stable polymorph of CaCO3 and the main component of limestone, marble, and chalk. Calcite exhibits double refraction (birefringence) and is used in optical instruments. Mohs hardness 3. It dissolves in dilute acids.]

bde8dfc934c35837f5279c15ed0119b784a7562e.lexicon: [Crystal: Halite], [Halite (rock salt) is the mineral form of sodium chloride (NaCl) with a cubic crystal structure. It is the most common evaporite mineral and is mined for table salt, road de-icing, and chemical production. Halite has a Mohs hardness of 2.5, density 2.165 g/cm3, and is highly soluble in water. It has a characteristic salty taste.]

cb6000409c0ff7ce52cbe47f62e02cdbbe3c348c.lexicon: [Crystal: Pyrite], [Pyrite (iron disulfide, FeS2) is a common iron sulfide mineral with a cubic crystal structure. Known as "fool's gold" due to its metallic lustre and golden colour. Mohs hardness 6-6.5, density 5.01 g/cm3. Pyrite is used in the production of sulfuric acid and as a semiconductor. It is the most abundant sulfide mineral.]

1d88c29b222155ad130acc9bc331018ca852e335.lexicon: [Crystal: Magnetite], [Magnetite (Fe3O4) is a strongly magnetic iron oxide mineral with a cubic crystal structure. It is the most magnetic of all naturally occurring minerals and is used as a compass needle material. Mohs hardness 5.5-6.5, density 5.17 g/cm3. Magnetite is an important iron ore and is used in magnetic recording media and ferrofluids.]

205398e90aac0db0ebb3d43d820a355cba4dbf2d.lexicon: [Math: Speed of Light], [The speed of light in vacuum (c) is a fundamental physical constant equal to exactly 299792458 metres per second. It is the maximum speed at which information or matter can travel. The constancy of c is a postulate of special relativity. All massless particles (photons, gluons, gravitons) travel at c. It defines the metre via SI units.]

b7bd70c5eda68f9c1e962d2b621c99f9e7a13ea2.lexicon: [Math: Planck Constant], [The Planck constant (h) is a fundamental physical constant equal to 6.62607015e-34 joule-seconds. It relates the energy of a photon to its frequency: E = hf. The reduced Planck constant (hbar = h/2pi) is used in quantum mechanics. The Planck constant defines the quantum of action and is central to quantum theory.]

ec7e8ad7beea49f37854b0381b859056ba86a176.lexicon: [Math: Gravitational Constant], [The gravitational constant (G) is a fundamental physical constant equal to 6.67430e-11 N m^2 kg^-2. It appears in Newton's law of gravitation: F = G m1 m2 / r^2. G also appears in Einstein's field equations of general relativity. It is the least precisely measured fundamental constant.]

ab8a74a1fb52476a85c27087cf12438171f16245.lexicon: [Math: Boltzmann Constant], [The Boltzmann constant (kB) is a fundamental physical constant equal to exactly 1.380649e-23 joules per kelvin. It relates the average kinetic energy of particles to temperature: E = (3/2) kB T. The Boltzmann constant defines the kelvin via SI units and appears in statistical mechanics, thermodynamics, and the ideal gas law.]

237c8254487fac04891e9336f756dc9b34ea42b4.lexicon: [Math: Elementary Charge], [The elementary charge (e) is the electric charge carried by a single proton, equal to exactly 1.602176634e-19 coulombs. It is the fundamental unit of electric charge. The electron carries charge -e. The elementary charge defines the ampere via SI units and appears in all electromagnetic phenomena involving charged particles.]

37791516806fbe3ac3307e3718f91845b3abec76.lexicon: [Math: Fine Structure Constant], [The fine structure constant (alpha) is a dimensionless fundamental constant approximately equal to 1/137.036. It characterises the strength of the electromagnetic interaction. Alpha = e^2 / (4 pi epsilon_0 hbar c). Its value determines the scale of atomic structure and the splitting of spectral lines. It is one of the most precisely measured constants.]

6698e12d3ee66fccc66f8a378c26216e0104242f.lexicon: [Math: Avogadro Constant], [The Avogadro constant (NA) is a fundamental physical constant equal to exactly 6.02214076e23 per mole. It is the number of constituent particles (atoms, molecules, ions) in one mole of a substance. NA defines the mole via SI units and connects macroscopic and atomic scales. It is named after Amedeo Avogadro.]

46860c6a91a370b695ff81599181e39405f33309.lexicon: [Math: Electron Mass], [The electron mass (me) is a fundamental physical constant equal to 9.1093837015e-31 kilograms or 0.51099895 MeV/c^2. It is the rest mass of the electron. The proton-to-electron mass ratio is 1836.15. The electron mass sets the scale of atomic physics and chemistry. It appears in the Bohr model, Schrodinger equation, and QED.]

cd9aa4481c6060817254a8fc6abd1323ef270148.lexicon: [Math: Proton Mass], [The proton mass (mp) is a fundamental physical constant equal to 1.67262192369e-27 kilograms or 938.272 MeV/c^2. It is the rest mass of the proton. The proton-to-electron mass ratio is 1836.15267343. The proton mass is approximately 1 atomic mass unit (u = 1.66054e-27 kg). It sets the scale of nuclear physics.]

10be15476bd4996c920dff316226526f58360190.lexicon: [Reaction: Photosynthesis], [Photosynthesis is the process by which plants, algae, and cyanobacteria convert light energy into chemical energy. The overall equation is: 6CO2 + 6H2O + light → C6H12O6 + 6O2. It occurs in two stages: the light reactions (in the thylakoid membrane) and the Calvin cycle (in the stroma). Photosynthesis is the primary source of oxygen in Earth's atmosphere.]

1d96bc906237d7a574865768d03a349cbfa54fe2.lexicon: [Reaction: Cellular Respiration], [Cellular respiration is the process by which cells break down glucose to produce ATP. The overall equation is: C6H12O6 + 6O2 → 6CO2 + 6H2O + ATP. It occurs in three stages: glycolysis (cytoplasm), the Krebs cycle (mitochondrial matrix), and oxidative phosphorylation (inner mitochondrial membrane). Produces ~30-32 ATP per glucose.]

514f38791dc5be28141353d03bd22fd66cc65f6c.lexicon: [Reaction: Haber-Bosch Process], [The Haber-Bosch process synthesises ammonia from nitrogen and hydrogen: N2 + 3H2 → 2NH3. It operates at 400-500 degrees C and 150-300 bar pressure using an iron catalyst. It is the most important industrial chemical reaction, producing ~150 million tonnes of ammonia per year for fertilisers. It consumes ~1-2% of global energy.]

86afe959e3668b104bb28ca6539abcb737c93bd9.lexicon: [Reaction: Methane Combustion], [The combustion of methane is the primary reaction in natural gas burning: CH4 + 2O2 → CO2 + 2H2O. It releases 890 kJ/mol of energy. This reaction powers gas turbines, heating systems, and is the basis of natural gas as a fuel. Complete combustion produces only CO2 and water; incomplete combustion produces CO and soot.]

39c45b092781e60fb72c7dea60ba6ed796b98113.lexicon: [Reaction: Electrolysis of Water], [The electrolysis of water splits water into hydrogen and oxygen using electrical energy: 2H2O → 2H2 + O2. It requires a minimum voltage of 1.23 V. This reaction is used to produce hydrogen gas for fuel cells and industrial processes. It is the reverse of the hydrogen fuel cell reaction. Efficiency is typically 60-80%.]

006c3dc50f9b8a76a50e3d34589d084c92f1f956.lexicon: [Reaction: Nuclear Fission of U-235], [Nuclear fission of uranium-235 occurs when a neutron is absorbed: U-235 + n → Ba-141 + Kr-92 + 3n + 200 MeV. The three released neutrons can trigger further fissions (chain reaction). This reaction is the basis of nuclear power plants and atomic bombs. It releases ~200 MeV per fission, approximately 50 million times more energy than chemical combustion.]

9fece67fae15ed8402cd30360e1f6257e441334f.lexicon: [Reaction: Nuclear Fusion D-T], [Deuterium-tritium fusion: D + T → He-4 + n + 17.6 MeV. This is the most energetically favourable fusion reaction at achievable temperatures (~100 million K). It is the basis of hydrogen bombs and the target reaction for fusion energy research (ITER, NIF). Deuterium is abundant in seawater; tritium is produced from lithium-6.]
